title: Bee Removal in Morningside Heights | $350-$550
metaDescription: Professional bee removal services in Morningside Heights, Manhattan. Safe extraction & relocation. Serving Columbia University area & nearby buildings. Call for fast response!
intro: Morningside Heights residents and property managers face unique bee control challenges in this historic Manhattan neighborhood. From pre-war apartment buildings near Columbia University to brownstones along tree-lined streets, bees often establish hives in building facades, rooftops, and window frames. Best Pest Control NYC provides safe, humane bee removal services throughout Morningside Heights, protecting both residents and these important pollinators.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC specializes in Morningside Heights' diverse architecture, from Columbia University campus buildings to classic residential structures along Riverside Drive. Our certified technicians use eco-friendly methods to safely remove and relocate bee colonies while preventing future infestations in this densely populated academic neighborhood.
localContext: Morningside Heights' abundance of green spaces including Morningside Park and Riverside Park, combined with flowering trees along Broadway and Amsterdam Avenue, naturally attracts bee populations. The neighborhood's mix of historic buildings, university structures, and proximity to both parks creates ideal conditions for bees to establish hives in architectural crevices and rooftop areas.

faqs.question: How much does bee removal cost in Morningside Heights?
faqs.answer: Bee removal services in Morningside Heights typically range from $350 to $550, depending on the hive location, colony size, and accessibility. Rooftop hives on taller buildings or colonies within wall voids may require additional equipment and labor, affecting the final price.

faqs.question: How quickly can you respond to bee problems near Columbia University?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day or next-day service for bee removal emergencies in Morningside Heights. Given the high foot traffic near Columbia University and residential density, we prioritize rapid response to ensure student and resident safety while the bees are safely relocated.

faqs.question: Do you relocate bees or exterminate them?
faqs.answer: We prioritize humane bee relocation whenever possible. Our technicians safely extract the entire colony, including the queen, and transport them to approved apiaries outside the city. Extermination is only considered in rare cases where relocation poses safety risks or is structurally impossible.

faqs.question: Can bees damage older Morningside Heights buildings?
faqs.answer: Yes, bee colonies can cause structural damage to pre-war and historic buildings common in Morningside Heights. Hives built within walls or under roofing can lead to moisture damage, wood deterioration, and honey seepage that attracts other pests. Prompt removal prevents costly repairs and preserves your property's integrity.
